title: Reverse transcription, primer pools preparation and multiplex PCR steps for DENV1 serotype for genomic sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2nqra","text":"This step-by-step protocol describes the cDNA synthesis, primer pools preparation and multiplex PCR conditions with the main goal to sequence the complete genome of DENV1 serotype strains.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text:
{"blocks":[{"key":"6q2ge","text":"Reagents:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"711d5","text":"
Reverse transcription: SuperScript™ IV First-Strand Synthesis System. (200 reactions) Cat:
18091200 Invitrogen","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b2hcq","text":"
Multiplex PCR: Q5® High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix. Cat: M0492L NEB, H20 Ultre Pure,
primers described in table 1.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Using a 2mL tube prepare the Mix 1 described below for 96 samples: A B C 1 Mix 1Reverse transcription Vol. (1x) 96 samples (+2 = 98 to keep some extra due to pipetting issues) 2 Random Hexamers (50µM) 1µL 98µL 3 dNTPs mix (10mM each) 1µL 98µL 4 Total 2µL 194µL

Using 0,2mL PCR tubes or 96 wells plates add 11-16µL of extracted RNA fromRT-PCR positive samples. Add 2µL of Mix 1 to the tube/well and take it to thethermocycler with the following set up 65ºC ---- 5 minutes
Take the tubes/wells to ice for 1 minute (you can prepare a water bath with ice cubes to have a uniform temperature distribution).

Using a 2mL tube prepare Mix 2 : A B C 1 Mix 2Reverse Transcription Vol.(1x) 96 samples (+2 = 98 to keepsome extra due to pipettingissues) 2 5x SSIV Buffer 4µL 392µL 3 100mM DTT 1µL 98µL 4 RNaseOUT orRNase Inhibitor 1µL 98µL 5 SSIV Reverse Transcriptase 1µL 98µL 6 Total 7µL 686µL
Add 7µL of Mix 2 to the tubes containing the Mix 1 plus RNA and take it to the thermocycler following the set up below: Step1:42ºC ---- 50 minutes70ºC ---- 10 minutes4ºC ---- Hold

Store the cDNA at -20ºC. Observation:. As a suggestion, to improve the final results only samples RT-PCR positive showing a Ct value of < 30 should be used for cDNA conversion and genomic amplification.
Select two 0,6mL tubes for each pool.

Prepare the Mix 1 for a Multiplex PCR for each Pool 1 and Pool 2 using a Falcon tubeof 15mL (~96 amostras) or a 2mL tube. A B C D 1 Mix 1Multiplex PCR Vol. Pool 1(1x) Vol. Pool 2(1x) 96 samples (+2) (pool1 or pool2) 2 Q5 Master Mix Highfidelity 2X 12,5 µl 12,5 µl 1.225 µl 3 Pool primers (Pool1 ouPool2) /Use concentration/ 1,5 µl 1,5 µl 147 µl 4 Ultra Pure Water 8,5 µl 8,5 µl 833 µl 5 Total 22,5µl 22,5µl 2205µl
Using the original 100uM primer solution eluted individually, put them together following the table below containing each primer volume.
Pool 1 will have a final volume of 130µl and pool 2 of 170µl.

In order to prepare the solution to use in the Multiplex PCR, dilute each pool 1:10. That is, 10µl of pool 1 and 90µl of ultrapure water. Note: The primers were designed using the https://primalscheme.com (Brito, 2021) based onthe JX669463.1 and KP188568 reference genomes.

Add 2,5µl of cDNA (totalling 5µl) in 22,5µl of the pool1 and pool2 reaction and takeit to the thermocycler following the conditions bellow: Step1:98ºC ---- 30 seconds Step2: (45 cycles)98ºC ---- 15 seconds58ºC ---- 30 seconds72ºC ---- 5 minutesStep3:72ºC ---- 2 minutesHold 4ºC
